questions.q: What is the role of emulsifiers in the preparation of emulsions?
questions.answer: Emulsifiers reduce the interfacial tension between two immiscible liquids, allowing them to mix and form a stable emulsion.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a food preservative? (A) Sodium benzoate (B) Sodium chloride (C) Sodium carbonate (D) Sodium sulphate
Write the chemical equation for the preparation of aspirin.
Fill in the blank: The antiseptic property of dettol is due to the presence of _______________________.
What is the difference between a disinfectant and an antiseptic? Give one example of each.
questions.answer: A disinfectant is used to kill microorganisms on non-living surfaces, while an antiseptic is used to kill microorganisms on living tissues. Example of disinfectant: bleach, example of antiseptic: dettol.

questions.q: State True or False: All antacids are bases.
Explain the mechanism of action of anti-histamines.
questions.answer: Anti-histamines work by blocking the histamine receptors in the body, thereby preventing the allergic response. They compete with histamine for binding to the receptor sites, reducing the allergic symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

questions.q: Which of the following is a synthetic food additive? (A) Vitamin C (B) Citric acid (C) Tartrazine (D) All of these
What are artificial sweeteners? Give one example.
questions.answer: Artificial sweeteners are synthetic compounds used as a substitute for sugar. Example: saccharin.

questions.q: Write a note on the importance of chemically treated sewage water in everyday life.
questions.answer: Chemically treated sewage water is important in everyday life as it helps to prevent the spread of water-borne diseases. The treatment process involves the removal of pollutants and pathogens, making the water safe for reuse in irrigation, industrial processes, and even drinking water supply. This helps to conserve water resources and reduce the burden on freshwater sources.
